source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the image, there is a circular loop next to a current-carrying solenoid. To induce a current \( i \) in the loop in the direction shown, which of the following actions would achieve this?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: Move the current-carrying solenoid away from the loop.

choice_map.A: Move the current-carrying solenoid closer to the loop.
choice_map.B: Move the current-carrying solenoid away from the loop.
choice_map.C: Increase the current in the solenoid.
choice_map.D: Insert an iron core into the solenoid.

extraction_notes: image field is an in-memory PIL image representation, not a path or URL
raw_answer_text mapped from solution letter B using choices in problem text

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'As shown in the image'
correct action depends on the shown current direction and spatial relation between loop and solenoid, which are not fully specified in text alone
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows an existing image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving requires combining the diagram's indicated current direction with electromagnetic induction/Lenz's law
the options vary different physical interventions (move closer, move away, increase current, insert iron core), so the solver must compare how each changes magnetic flux
this is more than direct lookup but still a relatively short conceptual chain rather than a long calculation
verifiability: choices provide a closed set of 4 candidate actions
answer_text exactly matches one listed option ('Move the current-carrying solenoid away from the loop.')
registry_passed=true with no listed asset issues, supporting stable downstream checking
